shared_properties: The light core occupies a 32x32 px source body that fits the GDD emitter footprint.
Core palette metadata remains primary #C9A84C, background #0D0B14, and accent #D4682A while beam-white pulses stay inside specs.colors_hex.
The silhouette stays readable even when the actual beam cone is rendered by a separate mask system.
differences: Six frames pulse the central lamp and shutter fins to communicate active beam emission and rail motion readiness.
Brightest frames intensify an exposure-white core ring while preserving darker housing edges for contrast.
Small amber shutter marks imply directed output without painting the entire beam into the source sprite.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds emitter size, moving-light behavior, and exposure danger sourced by active beams.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds bright amber and white light treatment against a dark tower palette.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds player-to-emitter scale for readable hazard proximity.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the light-core atlas during FloorLoading and use beam-mask materials for projected hazard areas.
runtime_notes: Advance the pulse animation at 6 fps while the emitter is active.
Render the source sprite beneath beam masks.
Keep the source pivot centered for rail motion and focused cone placement.

context.usage: Animated source body used for fixed, moving, or focused light emitters that define hazard beams and puzzle paths.

emission_notes: Pulse bloom never exceeds the 32 px canvas.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 192x32 px with 6 frames of 32x32 px and 0 px spacing.
The source body clearly reads as a dangerous active emitter even before the beam mask is rendered.
Pulse intensity changes remain readable without flattening the lamp housing silhouette.
